# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 153

This is turn 153. Turns 1 through 152 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 153.

## Turn 153 — universal machine certificate for the \(h_0\) two-jet

The frozen submission remains unchanged. Its byte-exact serialization and hash were fixed in Turn 149, while Turn 152 independently verified its marked maximal pro-\(2\) boundary.  

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
g0 = sigma2^2
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0
dg = d0^g0
hcomm = dg^-1*d0^-1*dg*d0
h0 = (x0^g0)*x0*dg*d0*d0^2*hcomm

h0*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit, \(x_0,x_1\) retain the stipulated 2-core semantics, and \(40491355905\) is the verifier residue of \(\omega_2\). 

Rather than test another isolated finite group, I built a fresh exact-word auditor for the complete class-two interface consumed by Propositions II and III.

### Universal collector identity

In the free nilpotent class-two, exponent-four group on the formal elements
\(\phi(X),X,\phi(D),D\), the script collects the literal \(h_0\)-word and verifies

\[
\begin{aligned}
h_0
&=\phi(X)X\phi(D)DD^2[\phi(D),D]\\
&=\phi(X)XD^{-1}\phi(D).
\end{aligned}
\]

This independently reproduces the structural identity first derived in Turn 134. 

It then verifies both projector branches:

\[
P=1:\quad h_0=X^2,
\]

and, when \(P=0\) so that \(D\) differs centrally from \(X\),

\[
h_0=\phi(X)^2=X^2
\]

for every square-preserving coefficient automorphism.

### Exhaustive coefficient probes

The implementation then evaluated the exact group words—not merely their formal Hessians—on three uniform families:

| Probe family | Actions exhausted | Exact evaluations |
|---|---|---:|
| Mixed Heisenberg groups, dimensions \(1,2,3\) | every element of \(\mathrm{GL}_n(\mathbf F_2)\) | 65,112 |
| Extraspecial groups, dimensions \(2,4\), both Arf classes | every quadratic isometry | 18,624 |
| Linked \(h_0c_0\) contribution | every relevant 2-power-order action | 22,896 |
| **Total** |  | **106,632** |

Every evaluation passed.

For the linked word, with

\[
U=S^{\omega_2},\qquad
\phi=U^{-2},\qquad
\psi=U^{-1},
\]

the exact central coordinates were:

\[
h_0c_0
\longmapsto
\lambda\!\left((1+U+U^{-1})c\right)
\]

in every mixed Heisenberg probe, and

\[
h_0c_0
\longmapsto
q(c)+b(c,U^{-1}c)
\]

in every extraspecial probe. These are precisely the natural mixed-pairing and determinant quadratic functors required by the repaired source interface. In particular, the replacement of the obsolete \(x_0^2\)-block by \(h_0\) introduces no hidden class-two correction.

Artifacts:

[Byte-exact frozen submission](sandbox:/mnt/data/q2_galois_h0_submission.txt)

[Turn 153 audit script](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn153_h0_class2_audit.py)

[Turn 153 JSON report](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn153_h0_class2_audit_report.json)

The submission remains 256 bytes with SHA-256

```text
4f81b04ae00e737a244f2445d70b095333a427508958f11489e3cabdc0192fdc
```

**ESTABLISHED**: Independently machine-certified the universal class-two identity for \(h_0\) and the exact linked \(h_0c_0\) two-jet. Exhausted 106,632 mixed-Heisenberg and extraspecial evaluations over all indicated automorphisms, isometries, central twists, and relevant 2-primary Frobenius actions. No correction to the frozen word is indicated.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun, and this certificate does not replace independent review of the full target-recurrence manuscript.

**NEXT**: Extend the same exact-word engine to a machine-readable chain-level certificate for Proposition II—deriving both relator differentials and their adjoint naturality for arbitrary elementary module maps—rather than opening another isolated finite target.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
